relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The high priest asks if the accusations are true.
Stephen begins with the Divine of glory appearing to Abraham before the land.
Abraham is called out toward a shown land.
Movement and promise precede settled possession.
Promise is given before inheritance or child.
The seed will sojourn and suffer in another land.
Judgment and exodus will lead into service.
Covenant passes through Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and the twelve.
Joseph is rejected by jealousy, yet the Divine is with him.
Grace and wisdom lift Joseph through pressure.
Famine exposes need.
Jacob sends the fathers to Egypt.
The rejected brother is revealed on the second encounter.
Joseph gathers the family into Egypt.
Jacob and the fathers die there.
The ancestral burial memory is recalled.
Promise-time draws near as the people multiply.
A king arises who does not know Joseph.
Empire turns crafty against vulnerable infants.
Moses is born beautiful to the Divine.
The exposed child is raised in Pharaoh’s house.
Moses grows powerful in Egyptian wisdom, words, and works.
At forty, his heart turns toward his siblings.
He defends the oppressed one.
He expects them to see deliverance, but they do not.
He urges peace between fighting siblings.
The wrongdoer rejects him as ruler and judge.
His prior violence is thrown back at him.
Moses becomes a sojourner in Midian.
After forty years, fire in the bush reveals holy encounter.
Wonder draws Moses toward the voice.
The ancestral Divine speaks from the fire.
Holy ground requires loosened sandals.
The Divine sees, hears, descends, rescues, and sends.
The rejected Moses becomes sent ruler and redeemer.
He leads through signs in Egypt, sea, and wilderness.
Moses points to the prophet like him.
In the wilderness assembly, living oracles are received.
The fathers refuse obedience and turn hearts back to Egypt.
They demand visible gods to go before them.
The calf becomes celebration of their own hands.
The Divine gives them over to chosen astral service.
Idol-tents and star-forms lead toward exile.
The tent of witness follows the heavenly pattern.
The tent is carried through Joshua into the land.
David asks for a dwelling.
Solomon builds a house.
The Most High is not contained in handmade houses.
Heaven and earth already form throne and footstool.
The Divine hand made all.
Stephen names resistance to holy Spirit in heart and ear.
Prophets and the Righteous One are resisted in the same pattern.
Law received through messengers was not guarded.
The speech cuts hearts and provokes rage.
Full of holy Spirit, Stephen sees glory and Jesus standing.
He beholds opened heavens and the Son of Humanity at the right hand.
They close ears and rush upon him.
Stephen is cast outside and stoned, with Saul watching the garments.
Stephen entrusts his spirit to Lord Jesus.
He releases the attackers from the charge as he falls asleep.
